notes: Add new shows here without touching any Python logic. Fields: id, name, genre, size (1 or 2), cost, upkeep, base_views, star_slots, ad_slots, rec_slots (list of 0-3), desc.

monopoly.desc: Laugh Factory  -  all show upkeep cut by 50% & +$8 Income per season. Low overhead, high laughs.
Drama Dynasty  -  all shows x1.22 Views & +$18 Income per season. Prestige programming commands a premium.
Sci-Fi Stranglehold  -  star view bonuses amplified x2.5 across ALL slots (not just Prime Time) & +1.10x Views & +$10 Income. The talent carries the universe.
Reality Lock  -  all ad seasonal income x1.50 & +1.08x Views & +$6 Income per season. Brands can't stop watching.
Sports Monopoly  -  all shows x1.30 Views & +$20 Income per season. Highest view multiplier in the game. Nobody changes the channel.
News Dominance  -  next milestone target x0.88 (easier quota) & +$5 Income per season. The facts are on your side.
Culinary Empire  -  +$22 DIRECT BUDGET per season (bypasses upkeep, added straight to budget) & +1.10x Views. Sponsorships pour in. The network smells like butter.

wildcard.desc: A blank slate. Name it, choose its genre, and pick its recommended time slot. Anything could air here  -  and probably will.

shows.name: The Cubicle
shows.desc: Documentary crew follows office workers in their natural habitat. Printer malfunction causes existential crisis. Somehow ran 9 seasons.

shows.name: Acquaintances
shows.desc: Six unemployed adults share a luxury Manhattan apartment on a barista's salary. It is absolutely Friends, down to the Central Perk couch and the Ross hair.

shows.name: North Lawn
shows.desc: Animated fourth-graders say things no child has ever said. Budget: $11. Writers: chaotic.

shows.name: The Bronze Gals
shows.desc: Four retirees eat cheesecake and destroy each other emotionally. Betty White would approve.

shows.name: Park Place
shows.desc: Idealistic city planner meets the concept of budgets. 'Treat yo self' is the only economic policy.

shows.name: Elementary, My Dear
shows.desc: New teacher at a chronically underfunded school. The WiFi is the final boss. The kids are alright.

shows.name: What's The Way?
shows.desc: A man spends 9 seasons telling his kids how he met their mother. The answer will disappoint everyone.

shows.name: Smart Guys, Dumb Problems
shows.desc: Physicists have social awkwardness played for laughs every 22 minutes. It is the Big Bang Theory, and Bazinga is still not a word.

shows.name: Nine-Nien!
shows.desc: A precinct that solves crimes and also its own HR violations. Brooklyn Nine-Nine, but louder.

shows.name: Laugh Track 3000
shows.desc: The audience laughs even when the joke is about taxes. Zero star slots, maximum ad revenue potential.

shows.name: Budget Buddies
shows.desc: Office pals dodge HR while the copier applies for PTO.

shows.name: Fixing Good
shows.desc: Chemistry teacher makes catastrophically bad life choices for 5 seasons. A cautionary tale of pride and chemistry gone wrong.

shows.name: Rules & Regs
shows.desc: DUN-DUN. 'In the criminal justice system...' is the most comforting sentence in television history.

shows.name: Musical Chairs
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT EPIC. Dragons, betrayal, and a finale that will make you unfollow dragons forever. The Red Wedding is still trending.

shows.name: The Successors
shows.desc: Wealthy dysfunctional family argues about who inherits the company. Everyone is awful. Nobody changes. We love it.

shows.name: Cream Stone
shows.desc: Sun-drenched resort. Guests check in with secrets; check out with trauma. Buffet is complimentary.

shows.name: The Bear Trap
shows.desc: Michelin-starred chef runs a failing beef sandwich shop. Every episode someone screams YES CHEF for 40 minutes.

shows.name: Agents of S.H.A.M.P.O.O.
shows.desc: Government agents fight supervillains while dealing with budget cuts and HR complaints. Clean espionage.

shows.name: Throne of Bones
shows.desc: Everyone wants the chair. The chair wants back.

shows.name: Midnight Court
shows.desc: Late-night verdicts, loud gavel ASMR.

shows.name: Boardroom Bloodlines
shows.desc: A dying patriarch pits his three children against each other in a live-broadcast shareholder meeting. Succession, but louder.

shows.name: Breaking Point
shows.desc: A forensic accountant discovers his firm launders money for a drug cartel. Paperwork has never been this tense.

shows.name: Star Path
shows.desc: Crew of a generational colony ship argues about governance for 7 seasons. Nobody reaches the planet. Critics love it.

shows.name: Android Blues
shows.desc: An android cop questions its own humanity while solving crimes in a rain-soaked megacity. Blade Runner, but with union benefits.

shows.name: Hero Complex
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. Superheroes behave badly and satirize capitalism. Funded by a multinational corporation.

shows.name: Cyberslug vs. Mecho Earthworm
shows.desc: An animated battle for the ages. Somehow better storytelling than most dramas. The earthworm is complex.

shows.name: Space Carpool Karaoke
shows.desc: Zero gravity, maximum harmonies, zero seatbelts.

shows.name: Mecha Farm
shows.desc: Giant robots harvest corn and existential dread.

shows.name: Signal Noise
shows.desc: An alien hijacks the local affiliate to complain about ad targeting.

shows.name: Quantum Telethon
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. A charity stream that accidentally opens a portal. Pledge drives will never be the same.

shows.name: The Outcaster
shows.desc: Strangers vote each other off an island over rice portions. Democracy in action.

shows.name: Kitchen Yelling
shows.desc: Scottish man screams at raw risotto. It is Hell's Kitchen with Gordon Ramsay at maximum volume.

shows.name: Squid League
shows.desc: Debt-ridden contestants play deadly children's games. Squid Game, but with network standards & practices screaming in the control room.

shows.name: Drag My Race
shows.desc: Charisma, Uniqueness, Nerve, and a craft budget of $50. RuPaul's Drag Race would absolutely read this lineup.

shows.name: Breath Mints Around the Globe
shows.desc: A man travels the world reviewing breath mints. Renewed for 7 seasons. Nobody asked why.

shows.name: The Great Bake-Off-By-One
shows.desc: One baker short, twice the panic. Mary Berry was unavailable. The tent remains.

shows.name: Infinite Garage
shows.desc: Collectors hoard every nostalgia toy ever printed. The garage grows. The family does not approve.

shows.name: Sweeps Week House
shows.desc: Contestants fight for the last working Nielsen box. The winner gets a cameo in a different show.

shows.name: Sunday Concussions
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. Men collide at high speed with profound legal liability for the league.

shows.name: Steel Chair Spectacular
shows.desc: Theatrical combat featuring folding chairs, dramatic entrances, and outcomes predetermined by writers who absolutely exist.

shows.name: Premier Kicking
shows.desc: Men fall over in agony. VAR overturns the goal. Fans weep into their pints. Repeated 38 weeks per year.

shows.name: Good Lad
shows.desc: An impossibly optimistic American coaches a failing soccer team. It is Ted Lasso in all but name, with Jason Sudeikis levels of wholesomeness.

shows.name: Ultimate Fainting Championship
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. Competitive synchronized fainting. A sport that deserves more respect than it receives.

shows.name: GrappleMania
shows.desc: The annual championship event. Pyrotechnics, entrance themes, and a main event that goes two hours over schedule.

shows.name: Chessboxing Nights
shows.desc: Checkmate, uppercut, repeat. A legitimate sport that demands both brain and brawn.

shows.name: Office Overtime Olympics
shows.desc: Ergonomic chair racing and high-stakes stapling finals. The water-cooler relay is genuinely suspenseful.

shows.name: Meteor Bowl XL
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. An interstellar championship with a suspiciously on-time meteor. Halftime show to follow.

shows.name: The Evening Briefing
shows.desc: Sober anchor, three graphs, and sponsored content dressed up as analysis. Runs every weeknight. Nobody has ever missed an episode.

shows.name: Global Crisis Network
shows.desc: BREAKING NEWS. Always breaking news. There has been a crisis every day for 40 years and GCN was there for all of them.

shows.name: The Anchor
shows.desc: A prime-time personality-driven newscast. The anchor has opinions. They are not hiding them. Ratings surge whenever there is controversy.

shows.name: Weather or Not
shows.desc: The forecast is always partly cloudy. The meteorologist is always confident. These two facts have never aligned.

shows.name: The Spin Zone
shows.desc: Two pundits argue about whether arguing is productive. Nobody wins. Both networks claim victory. Ad revenue is unaffected.

shows.name: The Cooling Zone
shows.desc: A soft-spoken chef explains garlic butter in extreme detail every episode. The ASMR community has found its show. Renewed indefinitely.

shows.name: Fridge Wars
shows.desc: Contestants cook three-course meals from whatever is currently in America's refrigerators. Results have included peanut butter steak.

shows.name: Burnt Offerings
shows.desc: Home cooks attempt gourmet dishes with one broken oven and a smoke detector that has opinions on everything.

shows.name: Last Resort Restaurant
shows.desc: A celebrity chef opens a restaurant with network money and is shocked when it nearly fails.

shows.name: Celebrity Dry Run
shows.desc: A-list celebrities attempt to cook a single dish without assistance. Several have required building evacuation.

shows.name: The Culinary Games
shows.desc: An elimination cooking tournament where the prize is a restaurant, the judges are unhinged, and the host weeps every single finale.

shows.name: Server Down!
shows.desc: IT department of a company where technology actively hates everyone. The WiFi router is the main villain. It won a Writers Guild award.

shows.name: Golden Glitches
shows.desc: Retirees navigate smartphones, streaming passwords, and grandchildren. The sequel nobody asked for. It is better than the original.

shows.name: Suburban Spy Dad
shows.desc: A CIA field agent maintains an aggressively normal suburban cover. His cover currently involves coaching a U8 soccer team.

shows.name: Flatline & Fine
shows.desc: A hospital drama questioned for medical accuracy by three cardiologists and the showrunner's physician. Ran twelve seasons anyway.

shows.name: Legal Eagle Loophole
shows.desc: A defense attorney has never lost a case and has never once followed a rule. The bar association is looking into this.

shows.name: The Long Farewell
shows.desc: An extended family gathers after a patriarch's will goes missing. Everyone has a motive. Nobody thought to hire a lawyer.

shows.name: Algorithm & Blues
shows.desc: An AI is granted full network programming authority. Ratings improve by 22% immediately. Nobody is allowed to ask how.

shows.name: Moonbase Malfunction
shows.desc: EXTENDED 2-SLOT. A lunar colony's life support is managed by an AI with a customer-service protocol. Hold time: seventeen minutes.

shows.name: Survive My Family
shows.desc: Contestants spend 72 hours with someone else's dysfunctional family. The therapy bills are separate from the prize fund.

shows.name: Influencer Island
shows.desc: Forty influencers are stranded without WiFi. Day three: mass crying. Day seven: one discovers fire.

shows.name: Armchair QB Supreme
shows.desc: Retired coaches argue every play call from luxury recliners. The post-game analysis runs four hours. Advertisers love it.

shows.name: Esports at Noon
shows.desc: Competitive gaming broadcast mid-day for corporate wellness synergy. Energy drink sponsorships are structurally mandatory.

shows.name: Opinion Overdrive
shows.desc: Six pundits fight for one microphone. The winner changes the topic. The debate has never ended and never will.

shows.name: The Morning Panic
shows.desc: Everything is a crisis by 7 AM. A format perfected over forty years of morning television. The coffee is complimentary.

shows.name: Starfall Armada
shows.desc: A galaxy-spanning space opera with a cast of thousands and a VFX bill to match. Colossal numbers — if you can keep the lights on. Two slots of prestige and an upkeep bill that will haunt your ledger.

shows.name: Crown of Ash
shows.desc: Royal intrigue, dragons of allegory, and a shocking death per episode. Awards bait that bleeds money but commands a premium audience. No ad slots — too prestigious for commercials.

shows.name: Bargain Bin Bonanza
shows.desc: Contestants dig through a warehouse for hidden treasure. Cheap, low ceiling alone — but three ad slots make it a billboard with a pulse. An ad-stacker's dream.

shows.name: Gridiron Sunday Primetime
shows.desc: Live championship football in the marquee slot. The biggest audience on the dial — and the biggest rights fee. Every season the upkeep tries to bankrupt you and every season the numbers make it worth it.

shows.name: The Midnight Diner
shows.desc: Comfort food and quiet confessions served after hours. A warm, dependable mid-tier earner with a star slot for a celebrity chef and room for two sponsors.

shows.name: Breaking Point
shows.desc: Wall-to-wall breaking coverage with an anchor who never blinks. Reliable, ad-friendly, and slightly pricier to run than it looks — those satellite trucks aren't free.

shows.name: Laugh Riot Live
shows.desc: A live multi-cam taped before a studio audience. Modest alone, but TWO star slots make it a vehicle built for talent — load it up and watch it climb.

shows.name: Quantum Paradox
shows.desc: A cerebral time-loop thriller that demands a prestige slot and pays back hard once it ages into a cult favourite. No ad slots — zero interruptions mandated. High maintenance, high reward.
